title: Phonograph made of Charlie Dale whistling; first telephone in Leland; other businesses and how boys made in town
description: Charlie Dale of Leland: whistler, made phonograph record of whistling. Albert Lee's meat business; solo to NB Long, First telephone in Leland. Van Pelt's flour mill. Boys earned money doing jobs in town, cared for doctors' horses.

title: Why NB long came west; having a fruit ranch and it's failure; moving to Kendrick and the businesses opened there; Marvin working as a clerk and what he made
description: NB Long's move west, 1889 to Crescent, Idaho. Sold cedar shakes and posts. Move to Fruit ranch. Apples. Failure. Albert Lee helped Longs start meat business. Mrs. Long ran hotel. Freight line to Pierce. Move to Kendrick, bought house, butcher shop, dray line. Marvin worked in store, 1909-10. Families buying for the winter. Marvin worked for $45.00 a month clerking.

title: Kendrick streets in 1908, mud and dust
description: Kendrick streets in 1908, mud and dust

title: Not having any trees or water in 1908; Chinese-American laundries; Native Americans coming though in the summer
description: No trees in 1908. Locust trees planted. Trees from Martin Thomas. No water for yards,1908. Hulls. Flowers, Mrs. Long's flowers. Gene Chinaman, vegetable man. Gene sent back to China, but died on the way. Chinese laundries. Indians coming through in summer. Dry goods prices in early stores. Men's suits.

title: President Taft visits Kendrick; T. Roosevelt spoke in Moscow; Train service
description: President Taft visits Kendrick. T. Roosevelt spoke in Moscow. Train service. Seasonal shopping.

title: Being a blacksmith in town; different Methodist churches; rural post offices
description: Blacksmith, Mr. Hodges in Leland. Marvin made spoke wedges, Hodges made toys, loved children. Leland at its height. North and South Methodist churches. Rural post offices around Kendrick.
